data.title: CHAPTER 3 — READING A FIGMA FILE PROGRAMMATICALLY
blocks: A Figma file is a tree. Every visible object on the canvas is a node. Every invisible container — frames, groups, pages
Chapter 3 — Reading a Figma File Program — The tree has a consistent shape at the top:

S03.meta: // SLIDE 03 — COMPONENTS AND STYLES: TWO PLA

data.title: COMPONENTS AND STYLES: TWO PLACES EACH
blocks: Components appear in two places in the response, for two different purposes.
Components and Styles: Two Places Each — At the top level, components is a flat index — a map from component ID to metadata. Each entry has the component's name,

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — WHAT IS NOT IN THE RESPONSE

data.title: WHAT IS NOT IN THE RESPONSE.
blocks: Document node tree — Yes — document key
Components flat index — Yes — components key
Styles flat index — Yes — styles key
Variables and collections — Only on Enterprise plan — variables key
data.section: WHAT IS NOT IN THE RESPONSE

data.title: FIGMA-READ.MJS
blocks: const retryAfter = parseInt(res.headers.get('Retry-After') — '10', 10);
console.error(`Figma API error ${res.status} — ${body.err — 'unknown'}`);
const componentMap = data.components — {};
description meta.description — '',

data.title: THE LOCAL FIXTURE.
blocks: The fixture written by figma-read.mjs is not a nice-to-have. It is a core practice.
The Local Fixture — A full file fetch on a large design system takes several seconds and consumes rate-limit quota. Figma imposes rate limit

data.title: THREE READ STRATEGIES.
blocks: GET /v1/files/ key — Full audits, complete traversals, any analysis where you do not know in advance which nodes you need
GET /v1/files/ key?depth=N — Structural reads: page names, top-level frame types, checking node presence without traversing subtrees
GET /v1/files/ key/nodes?ids=ID1,ID2,... — Targeted extraction: you have a list of component IDs from a prior structural pass and need

data.title: THE OUTPUT SHAPE.
blocks: figma-read.mjs produces three files. These are the stable interface for everything downstream.
The Output Shape — component-inventory.json is a flat list of all component definitions: names, IDs, descriptions, and a boolean hasDescrip

data.title: FAILURE MODES
blocks: The large-file timeout.
The partial variable response.
Stale node IDs.
The archive page problem.
Instance vs. definition confusion.

data.title: WHAT COMES NEXT.
blocks: You can now read any Figma file programmatically. You have a fixture for offline development. You have a component inven
